author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
verses:
sadopāsyaḥ śrīmān dhṛta⋅manuja⋅kāyaiḥ praṇayitāṁ
vahadbhir gīrvāṇair giriśa⋅parameṣṭhi⋅prabhṛtibhiḥ
sva⋅bhaktebhyaḥ śuddhāṁ nija⋅bhajana⋅mudrām upadiśan
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

sureśānāṁ durgaṁ gatir atiśayenopaniṣadāṁ
munīnāṁ sarva⋅svaṁ praṇata⋅paṭalīnāṁ madhurimā
viniryāsaḥ premṇo nikhila⋅paśu⋅pālāmbuja⋅dṛśāṁ
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

svarūpaṁ vibhrāṇo jagad⋅atulam advaita⋅dayitaḥ
prapanna⋅śrīvāso janita⋅paramānanda⋅garimā
harir dīnoddhārī gaja⋅pati⋅kṛpotseka⋅taralaḥ
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

rasoddāmā kāmārbuda⋅madhura⋅dhāmojjvala⋅tanur
jatīnām uttaṁsas taraṇi⋅kara⋅vidyoti⋅vasanaḥ
hiraṇyānāṁ lakṣmī⋅bharam abhibhavann āṅgika⋅rucā
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

hare kṛṣṇety uccaiḥ sphurita⋅rasano nāma⋅gaṇanā⋅
kṛta⋅granthi⋅śreṇī⋅subhaga⋅kaṭi⋅sūtrojjvala⋅karaḥ
viśālākṣo dīrghārgala⋅yugala⋅khelāñcita⋅bhujaḥ
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

payorāśes tīre sphurad⋅upavanālī⋅kalanayā
muhur vṛndāraṇya⋅smaraṇa⋅janita⋅prema⋅vivaśaḥ
kvacit kṛṣṇāvṛtti⋅pracala⋅rasano⋅bhakti⋅rasikaḥ
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

rathārūḍhasyārād adhipadavi nīlācala⋅pater
adabhra⋅premormi⋅sphurita⋅naṭanollāsa⋅vivaśaḥ
sa⋅harṣaṁ gāyadbhiḥ parivṛta⋅tanur vaiṣṇava⋅janaiḥ
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

bhuvaṁ siñcann aśru⋅śrutibhir abhitaḥ sāndra⋅pulakaiḥ
parītāṅgo nīpa⋅stabaka⋅nava⋅kiñjalka⋅jayibhiḥ
ghana⋅sveda⋅stoma⋅stimita⋅tanur utkīrtana⋅sukhī
sa caitanyaḥ kiṁ me punar api dṛśor jāsyati padam

adhīte gaurāṅga⋅smaraṇa⋅padavī⋅maṅgalataraṁ
kṛtī jo viśrambha⋅sphurad⋅amala⋅dhīr aṣṭakam idam
parānande sadyas tad⋅amala⋅padāmbhoja⋅yugale
parisphārā tasya sphuratu nitarāṁ prema⋅laharī
en_translation:
Will that glorious Śrī Caitanya, who is always worshiped by demigods like Giriśa (Lord Śiva) and Parameṣṭhi (Lord Brahmā) and others, who assume human bodies out of affection, and who instructs His devotees in the pure method of His own worship, again become visible to my eyes?

Will that Śrī Caitanya, who is an inaccessible destination for the kings of the demigods, the special goal of the Upaniṣads, the very life of the sages, the sweetness for the multitude of surrendered souls, and the essence of love for all the lotus-eyed gopīs, again become visible to my eyes?

Will that Śrī Caitanya, who manifests a unique form in the world, the beloved of Advaita Ācārya, the protector of Prapanna Śrīvāsa, the source of supreme bliss and greatness, Hari, the deliverer of the fallen, and who is moved by the outpouring of grace to Gajapati (King Pratāparudra), again become visible to my eyes?

Will that Śrī Caitanya, who is full of ecstatic rasa, whose brilliant form is as sweet as millions of Cupids, the crest-jewel of ascetics, whose garments shine like sunbeams, and who overcomes the opulence of gold and Lakṣmī with the splendor of His bodily luster, again become visible to my eyes?

Will that Śrī Caitanya, whose tongue vibrates loudly chanting "Hare Kṛṣṇa," whose charming hand shines with a beautiful thread (mālā) for counting names, with a series of knots, who has broad eyes, and whose playfully moving arms resemble long bolts, again become visible to my eyes?

Will that Śrī Caitanya, who, on the shore of the ocean, seeing the shining rows of gardens, becomes repeatedly overwhelmed by love arising from the remembrance of Vṛndāvana, and who sometimes, with a restless tongue repeating Kṛṣṇa's names, is a connoisseur of devotional rasa, again become visible to my eyes?

Will that Śrī Caitanya, who, near the Ratha (chariot)-riding Lord of Nīlācala (Jagannātha Purī), becomes overwhelmed by boundless waves of love, manifesting ecstatic dancing, and whose body is surrounded by Vaiṣṇavas joyfully singing, again become visible to my eyes?

Will that Śrī Caitanya, who sprinkles the earth with streams of tears, whose body is covered all around with dense goosebumps surpassing the fresh pollen of a nīpa flower cluster, whose body is moistened by profuse streams of sweat, and who finds joy in loud chanting, again become visible to my eyes?

May the waves of love of that fortunate person, whose pure intelligence shines with faith, and who studies this aṣṭakam (eight verse prayer), the supremely auspicious path of remembering Gaurāṅga, spread abundantly and immediately in supreme bliss at His immaculate lotus feet.
